ei.headline: High-Growth Candidate
ei.context_note: Ei day: auspicious for career advancement
ei.detail: This candidate performs best in growth-oriented environments. Give them challenges and resources, and the returns will exceed expectations. Suited for positions requiring independent leadership and team elevation. The ambition and vision shown in interviews is genuine.
do: Assign challenging core projects
Provide a clear promotion path
Observe leadership potential and strategic vision in interviews
Grant sufficient decision-making authority post-hire
avoid: Placing them in positions with no growth potential
Over-constraining their initiative

shin.headline: Trainable Candidate
shin.context_note: Shin day: auspicious for forming bonds
shin.detail: This candidate is willing to accept company investment in their development, with high loyalty. Training costs will yield strong returns. Suited for core positions where the company is willing to invest long-term. Interviews typically go well, with trust building naturally between both sides.
do: Design a comprehensive training plan
Provide a mentor system
Evaluate learning willingness and stability in interviews
Offer a long-term development commitment
avoid: Offering only short-term contracts without development opportunities
Investing in training without follow-up tracking

yu.headline: Team Catalyst Candidate
yu.detail: This candidate's social skills and team-building ability are their greatest assets. They can quickly energize team morale and build cross-departmental relationships. Suited for coordination and communication roles. Watch for over-giving — their manager should proactively check on their wellbeing.
do: Assign roles requiring cross-functional collaboration
Observe team interaction skills in interviews
Regularly check their energy levels post-hire
Publicly recognize their contributions to team dynamics
avoid: Draining their social energy without reciprocating
Pushing all communication tasks onto one person

sui.headline: Steady Deep-Focus Candidate
sui.context_note: Sui day: suitable for recovery only
sui.detail: This candidate excels at solving existing problems and quality assurance. A specialist in obstacle removal. Not suited for greenfield initiatives, but delivers stable results in maintaining and improving existing systems. Let them focus on their strengths after hiring.
do: Assign quality assurance and problem-solving responsibilities
Support certification and skill development
Evaluate problem-solving ability in interviews
avoid: Assigning greenfield zero-to-one initiatives
Expecting them to proactively develop new business

an.headline: Foundation Builder Candidate
an.context_note: An day: auspicious for relocation and building
an.detail: This candidate excels at establishing processes and building foundations. Ideal for expanding teams that need structure. They can bring order to chaos but are not suited for already-mature teams. Process-building experience is a strong plus in interviews.
do: Place in new teams or departments under construction
Evaluate process design and system-building experience in interviews
Give ample time to establish roots
Value their infrastructure contributions
avoid: Placing them in mature teams doing routine work
Rushing short-term output expectations

kai.headline: Problem Solver Candidate
kai.context_note: Kai day: only for overcoming obstacles
kai.detail: This candidate shines when facing tough problems. Suited for teams with hard challenges to tackle. Routine daily work will bore them, but hand them a tough problem and they'll deliver strong results. Consider project-based or contract positions.
do: Assign projects with clear hard problems to solve
Use stress tests in interviews to evaluate resilience
Grant sufficient autonomy for problem-solving
Consider project-based or contract flexible arrangements
avoid: Assigning routine maintenance work
Expecting long-term stability in a predictable environment

ki.headline: Internal Relations Candidate
ki.context_note: Ki day: auspicious for gatherings, not for external deals
ki.detail: This candidate's social skills are best applied internally. Strong at building cross-departmental networks and nurturing team relationships. Not suited for positions requiring extensive external negotiation or travel. Teams that value internal culture are a strong match.
do: Assign roles focused on team dynamics and internal communication
Evaluate understanding of team culture in interviews
Involve in cross-departmental projects post-hire
avoid: Placing in frequent-travel or external-negotiation positions
Assigning large contract or external client management

sei.headline: Learning Achiever Candidate
sei.context_note: Sei day: auspicious for study and accomplishment
sei.detail: This candidate has strong learning ability and solid delivery skills. Thrives in companies with comprehensive training programs, able to continuously grow and produce results. Their learning track record and project completion rate are reliable interview indicators.
do: Provide comprehensive training resources
Focus interview evaluation on learning ability and achievement records
Assign tasks with clear delivery targets
Offer regular opportunities to acquire new skills
avoid: Expecting high output without providing learning resources
Judging only on past experience while ignoring learning potential

mei.headline: Culture Mirror Candidate
mei.context_note: Mei day: not suitable for major actions
mei.detail: This candidate is highly similar to the existing team — zero integration cost. But they won't bring new perspectives. Suited for teams needing stability, not for positions requiring innovation. Hiring them reinforces the existing culture; whether that's good depends on the team's current state.
do: Assess whether the team needs consistency or diversity right now
Place in roles requiring high team consensus
Pair with team members of different styles to balance homogeneity
avoid: Adding more of the same type to an already-homogeneous team
Expecting them to bring different perspectives or approaches

gyo.headline: Accumulation Phase Candidate
gyo.context_note: Gyo day: actions don't bear immediate fruit
gyo.detail: This candidate is currently in an energy accumulation phase — low short-term burst potential but building solid foundations. Suited for positions where immediate output isn't urgent and the company is willing to invest time in development. In a stable environment with time, they'll exceed expectations when their energy rebounds.
do: Assign steady-pace work that allows gradual accumulation
Provide time and space for learning and recharging
Don't pressure with short-term KPIs
Patiently wait for the energy rebound
avoid: Placing in high-pressure positions requiring immediate output
Evaluating with short-term performance metrics

tai.headline: Potential Development Candidate
tai.context_note: Tai day: not suitable for major actions
tai.detail: This candidate is a potential-type talent who develops best in mature systems. Suited for companies with comprehensive training programs to nurture their growth. Not suited for greenfield startup work. With an existing foundation and guidance, their potential will gradually emerge.
do: Place in positions with existing foundations to inherit
Provide comprehensive training and a mentor
Develop steadily within a mature team
Allow sufficient growth time
avoid: Assigning greenfield startup tasks
Rushing for short-term results
